title: unwrap() over into_iter().flatten(): fails closed, comment explains safety
description: The old as_object().into_iter().flatten() silently produced an empty iterator if the value was not an object — which would have silently skipped the unknown-key check (fail open, wrong behavior). The new as_object().unwrap() panics in that case (fail closed, better for security). The safety is guaranteed by the is_object() guard at line 486 that hits 'continue' before this code runs; the comment at line 539 documents this. A future contributor might suggest reverting to the defensive idiom — resist this; failing closed on an 'unreachable' path is more correct than silently skipping a security-relevant validation.
close_reason: Decision records — no code change needed; recorded for future reference
